varieties.name: Vietnamese White
varieties.traits: Pink skin with bright green fins, medium to large fruit (0.5-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh with small black seeds, mild sweet flavor with watermelon-like texture, tangy undertones
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy budding, very productive with 3-4 fruiting cycles per year in humid subtropical climates climate

varieties.name: Aussie Gold (Isis Gold, Bundaberg Gold)
varieties.origin: Israeli hybrid developed as 'Israeli Golden', adapted in Australia, Queensland (Bundaberg region)
varieties.traits: Bright yellow skin, thornless (unlike other yellow varieties), large fruit up to 1.5 kg
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, sweet lemonade flavor (BRIX 17-20), resembles sweet lemonade taste, kiwi-like texture
varieties.yield_notes: Aggressive grower, flowers quicker than most varieties, excellent productivity

varieties.name: Dark Star
varieties.origin: USA (Paul Thomson hybrid), Selenicereus guatemalensis x S. undatus
varieties.traits: Hot pink/dark pink skin with long thin bracts, medium to large fruit (0.75-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple/magenta flesh, sweet grape-like flavor (BRIX 19), white pulp with black seeds in some variants
varieties.yield_notes: Rapid growth, highly productive, requires cross-pollination

varieties.name: Columbian Supreme Red
varieties.traits: Pink to red skin with deep red flesh, larger fruit, thinner skin than other varieties
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red smooth flesh, sweet flavor (BRIX 15), tastes like quality shop-bought dragon fruit, fruit size 500-900g
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous grower, very heavy producer, one of main commercial varieties in Australia, flowers in late July (winter flowering capability)

varieties.name: Physical Graffiti
varieties.traits: Hot pink and green skin reminiscent of street art, large fruit (0.75-1.5 lbs, up to 680g)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark pink/magenta flesh, remarkably delicious, subtly sweet with creamy smooth texture, balanced sweet-sour taste
varieties.yield_notes: One of top producers, up to 4 fruiting cycles per year in warm climates, self-fertile (no hand pollination needed), prolific and easy to grow

varieties.name: Delight
varieties.traits: Bright pink skin, medium fruit (0.75-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink flesh, very sweet and flavorful
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer, abundant fruit set, early maturation, self-fertile
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, Rare Dragon Fruit, The Fruit Jungle Noosa, Daley's Fruit

varieties.name: Purple Haze
varieties.traits: Large fruit up to 2 lbs, purple-fleshed variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple flesh with relatively few seeds, pleasant grape and kiwi-like flavor, sweet (BRIX 19)
varieties.yield_notes: Very aggressive grower, requires cross-pollination (self-sterile)

varieties.name: Rixford Purple
varieties.traits: Purple-fleshed dragon fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple/magenta flesh, sweet flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, various Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Scott's Purple
varieties.traits: Purple-fleshed variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple flesh, sweet
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity

varieties.name: Honey White
varieties.traits: Pink skin, white-fleshed variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, honey-sweet flavor, sweeter than standard Vietnamese White
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer, self-fertile
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Lucille Lemonade
varieties.traits: Pink skin, unique flavor profile
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, distinctive lemonade-flavored fruit
varieties.yield_notes: Limited availability, still being trialed
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit (limited), Daley's Fruit (limited)

varieties.name: Aztec Gem
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit, purple flesh, brilliant flavor
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very deep purple flesh, sweet and fruity taste, fruit weight 300-400g
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate grower, self-pollinating, heavy flowering
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, Daley's Fruit, The Fruit Jungle Noosa

varieties.name: Pink Panther
varieties.traits: Named for bright colored flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright purple/pink flesh, nice balance of acidity and sweetness, refreshing
varieties.yield_notes: Good producer
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, various Queensland nurseries, Gumtree humid subtropical climates listings

varieties.name: Condor
varieties.traits: Light green and pink skin turning pinkish-red when ripe, large fruit (1-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Vibrant purple flesh, great tasting, bright purple color
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, excellent exterior appearance

varieties.name: Jungle Jim
varieties.traits: Unique variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor profile
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit

varieties.name: Exit
varieties.traits: Unique cultivar
varieties.flesh_fruit: Distinctive characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Variable
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit (limited)

varieties.name: Connie Mayer (Connie Meyer)
varieties.traits: White flesh, green skin, small fruit (0.5-0.75 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, great tasting, sweet coconut flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Highly productive with red-fleshed pollinator, self-sterile

varieties.name: Blush
varieties.traits: Pink/blush coloring
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light colored flesh
varieties.yield_notes: Recommend hand pollination for best results
varieties.availability: Bush to Creek Dragon Fruit, Queensland specialty farms

varieties.name: Voodoo Child
varieties.traits: Small fruit, slightly larger than an egg
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sangria-colored flesh, exceptionally flavorful, sweet grape-like taste
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, good for juice production

varieties.name: Yellow Megalanthus (Dwarf Prickly Yellow, Colombiana)
varieties.origin: Colombia, South America (native to Peru, Ecuador, Colombia)
varieties.traits: Yellow skin with spines/thorns, small oblong fruit (~5 oz), spines easy to remove when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, extremely sweet (BRIX 21), tastes similar to kiwi, aromatic, long ripening period (up to 180 days)
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, harvested late fall into winter (November-February)

varieties.name: David Bowie
varieties.traits: Pinkish-red skin with numerous green bracts, oval-shaped, medium to large fruit (~1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, sweet and tangy, extraordinary flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Strong growers, productive, self-fertile and self-pollinating, somewhat slow growing initially
varieties.availability: Specialty Queensland nurseries, international suppliers

varieties.name: American Beauty
varieties.traits: Red skin, round fruit (0.5-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep purple flesh, rich in antioxidants, sweet with tart aftertaste when picked early, very sweet when fully ripe
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, harvested June-November, less tolerant of full sun
varieties.availability: The Fruit Jungle Noosa, various Queensland nurseries, humid subtropical climates region suppliers

varieties.name: Zamorano
varieties.traits: Red skin with dark green fins, small to medium fruit (0.75-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crimson red flesh, sweet mild flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, huge flowers (over 14 inches), one of first to bloom, flowers all year, slower growth initially

varieties.name: Palora (Ecuador Palora)
varieties.traits: Bright yellow skin with spines (half the spines of regular Yellow Dragon), large fruit (1-1.5 lbs, 2-3x size of regular Yellow Dragon)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh with transparency hints, sweetest variety (BRIX 20-24), crunchy seeds, resembles Juicy Fruit candy, combination of pear and kiwi with floral notes
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, aggressive growth, more resilient than regular Yellow Dragon, 6 months to ripen, vigorous in spring/summer/early fall

varieties.name: Sugar Dragon
varieties.traits: Red skin, medium fruit (0.75 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purplish-red flesh, very sweet (BRIX 18-20), semi-firm texture
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, plentiful fragrant flowers, excellent universal pollinator, robust grower with meaty stems

varieties.name: Neon
varieties.traits: Red skin, medium-sized fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Stunning magenta flesh, abundant juicy fruit, tangy yet sweet grape-like flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, produces abundantly, beautiful large blooms, early season fruit, can set fruit throughout summer

varieties.name: Lisa
varieties.traits: Thick red skin with green bracts having dark red edges, artistic appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red flesh, sweet and slightly tangy, very flavorful
varieties.yield_notes: Self-sterile (needs cross-pollination), excellent pollinator producer, gray coating on stem for heat/cold protection

varieties.name: Edgar's Baby
varieties.traits: Green mature skin (difficult to determine ripeness, shows red bleeding when ripe), compact growth, fruit 0.75-1 lb
varieties.flesh_fruit: Pink/purple flesh, juicy sweetness (BRIX 19 when mature), unique variety
varieties.yield_notes: Harvest 50-65 days after blooming, better fruit in second year, early-fruiting
varieties.availability: The Fruit Jungle Noosa, specialty collectors (but not recommended for humid subtropical climates)

varieties.name: Natural Mystic
varieties.traits: Unique variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate

varieties.name: Cosmic Charlie
varieties.traits: Distinctive variety
varieties.flesh_fruit: Unique characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Good
varieties.availability: humid subtropical climates region Gumtree listings, Queensland growers

varieties.name: Kathy Van Arum
varieties.traits: Unique clone
varieties.flesh_fruit: Distinctive
varieties.yield_notes: Variable

varieties.name: Orange Giant
varieties.traits: Orange-colored skin, larger than Orange Dwarf
varieties.flesh_fruit: One of sweetest varieties, sweeter than Orange Dwarf
varieties.yield_notes: Easier to grow than Orange Dwarf, larger fruit
varieties.availability: The Fruit Jungle Noosa, specialty Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Halley's Comet
varieties.traits: Pink skin with green fins, large fruit (1.5-2.0 lbs), more rounded than Physical Graffiti
varieties.flesh_fruit: Beautiful purple flesh, similar to Physical Graffiti in other respects
varieties.yield_notes: Hearty plant, slow growing, tolerates full sun, requires cross-pollination
varieties.availability: Queensland specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Asunta
varieties.origin: Hybrid variety (H. stenopterus x H. guatemalensis)
varieties.traits: Unique Mexican hybrid
varieties.flesh_fruit: Distinctive flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Variable

varieties.name: Hylocereus Undatus (Vietnamese White, White Dragon Fruit) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Mexico and Central America, extensively cultivated in Vietnam, Thailand, and throughout Asia since 1990s. Widely grown in India.
varieties.traits: Pink to red skin with green fins, medium to large fruit (0.5-1.5 lbs), most common dragon fruit variety globally
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh with tiny black edible seeds, mildly sweet flavor, crisp texture similar to watermelon, refreshing
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, highly productive (8-13.5 tons/hectare in established plantations), multiple fruiting cycles, heavy budding

varieties.name: Hylocereus Costaricensis (Red Dragon Fruit, Pitaya Roja) 🌱
varieties.origin: Native to Costa Rica and Nicaragua, cultivated throughout tropical regions. Growing in India since early 2000s.
varieties.traits: Red to deep pink skin with green fins, medium to large fruit, violet-red to magenta flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red/magenta flesh, richer and sweeter than white-fleshed varieties, high in antioxidants (betalains), lycopene content
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, slightly sweeter than white-fleshed types, requires well-drained soil

varieties.name: Hylocereus Megalanthus (Yellow Dragon Fruit, Pitaya Amarilla)
varieties.origin: Native to Colombia, Ecuador, Peru, Bolivia (South America). Colombia is largest producer.
varieties.traits: Bright yellow skin with spines/thorns, oval shaped, fruit up to 10cm long, spines on scales that can be brushed off when ripe
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, sweetest variety (often BRIX 21-24), aromatic, similar to kiwi taste, crunchy edible black seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, longer ripening time, unique flavor niche market, less common than pink-skinned varieties

varieties.name: Palora (Ecuador Palora, Yellow Dragon)
varieties.origin: Palora, Ecuador. Cultivated commercially in Ecuador.
varieties.traits: Bright yellow skin with fewer spines than standard Megalanthus, large fruit (1-1.5 lbs, 2-3x size of regular Yellow)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh with transparency, sweetest of all varieties (BRIX 22-24), combination of pear and kiwi with floral notes, crunchy seeds
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, more vigorous than regular Megalanthus, 6 months to ripen, aggressive growth, resilient

varieties.name: Vietnamese White Giant 🌱
varieties.traits: Pink skin with green fins when ripe, larger than standard Vietnamese White
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, tangy with watermelon texture, small edible seeds, dramatic color contrast inside/outside
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, heavy budding (suggested to thin for larger fruit), night-blooming fragrant flowers

varieties.name: Physical Graffiti
varieties.traits: Hot pink and green skin, large fruit (0.75-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Dark pink/magenta flesh, remarkably delicious, subtly sweet with creamy texture
varieties.yield_notes: Top producer variety, self-fertile, up to 4 fruiting cycles per year in warm climates, prolific and easy to grow

varieties.name: American Beauty
varieties.traits: Red skin, round fruit (0.5-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep purple flesh, very sweet when ripe, rich in antioxidants (BRIX 18.51)
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, requires some shade, harvested June-November
varieties.availability: Available through specialty international suppliers. Can be sourced for Indian growers through online nurseries and specialty fruit suppliers.

varieties.name: Dark Star
varieties.traits: Dark pink skin with long thin bracts, medium to large fruit (0.75-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple/magenta flesh, sweet grape-like flavor (BRIX 19), hints of grape
varieties.yield_notes: Rapid growth, highly productive, requires cross-pollination (self-sterile)

varieties.name: Delight
varieties.traits: Bright pink skin, medium fruit (0.75-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Light pink flesh, very sweet and flavorful
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable producer, abundant fruit set, early maturation, self-fertile
varieties.availability: Available through specialty suppliers. Can be sourced for Indian growers through online nurseries and international suppliers.

varieties.name: Sugar Dragon
varieties.traits: Red skin, medium fruit (0.75 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purplish-red flesh, very sweet (BRIX 18-20), semi-firm texture
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, plentiful fragrant flowers, excellent universal pollinator, robust grower with meaty stems

varieties.name: Voodoo Child
varieties.traits: Small fruit, slightly larger than an egg
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sangria-colored flesh, exceptionally flavorful, sweet grape-like taste
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, good for juice production, best chilled

varieties.name: Neon
varieties.traits: Red skin, medium-sized fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Stunning magenta flesh, tangy yet sweet grape-like flavor, abundant and juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, produces abundantly, beautiful large blooms, early season fruit, can set fruit throughout summer
varieties.availability: Available through specialty international suppliers. Can be sourced for tropical monsoon climates through online nurseries.

varieties.name: Purple Haze
varieties.traits: Large fruit up to 2 lbs
varieties.flesh_fruit: Purple flesh with relatively few seeds, pleasant grape and kiwi-like flavor, sweet (BRIX 19)
varieties.yield_notes: Very aggressive grower, requires cross-pollination (self-sterile)
varieties.availability: Available through specialty suppliers. Can be sourced for tropical monsoon climates through international nurseries and online suppliers.

varieties.name: Lisa
varieties.traits: Thick red skin with green bracts having dark red edges, artistic appearance
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red flesh, sweet and slightly tangy, very flavorful
varieties.yield_notes: Self-sterile (requires cross-pollination), excellent pollen producer, gray stem coating for heat/cold protection

varieties.name: Columbian Supreme Red 🌱
varieties.traits: Pink to red skin, larger fruit (500-900g), thinner skin
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red smooth flesh, sweet (BRIX 15)
varieties.yield_notes: Vigorous grower, very heavy producer, self-fertile, can flower in winter
varieties.availability: Available through specialty suppliers. Growing in India in various states. Can be sourced for tropical monsoon climates through agricultural universities, specialty nurseries.

varieties.name: Aztec Gem
varieties.traits: Small to medium fruit (300-400g), purple flesh
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very deep purple flesh, sweet and fruity taste, brilliant flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate grower, self-pollinating, heavy flowering
varieties.availability: Available through specialty suppliers. Can be sourced for tropical monsoon climates through online nurseries.

varieties.name: Condor
varieties.traits: Light green and pink skin turning pinkish-red when ripe, large fruit (1-1.5 lbs)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Vibrant purple flesh, great tasting, bright purple color
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, excellent exterior appearance
varieties.availability: Available through specialty suppliers. Can be sourced for tropical monsoon climates through online nurseries and international suppliers.

varieties.name: Zamorano
varieties.traits: Red skin with dark green fins, small to medium fruit (0.75-1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: Crimson red flesh, sweet mild flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Self-fertile, huge flowers (14+ inches), one of first to bloom, flowers all year, excellent pollen source

varieties.name: David Bowie
varieties.traits: Pinkish-red skin with numerous green bracts, medium to large fruit (~1 lb)
varieties.flesh_fruit: White flesh, sweet and tangy, extraordinary flavor
varieties.yield_notes: Strong growers, productive, self-fertile and self-pollinating, somewhat slow growing initially

varieties.name: Vietnamese Jaina 🌱
varieties.traits: Large fruit (0.5-1.5 lbs), impressive size
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet snowy white flesh
varieties.yield_notes: Self-pollinating, popular among growers and consumers worldwide
